commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Feishu streaming card updates to send full accumulated text to CardKit and uses full element replacement during final closeout.

PR surface: Source -9. Total -9 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection plus Feishu's official docs show the likely failure path: current main sends suffix text where the CardKit streaming text API expects full text.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied live Feishu proof plus narrow code/test cleanup before normal maintainer review or merge can proceed.

target.title: fix(feishu): restore full-text streaming card updates to prevent content truncation
target.body:
## Summary

- Streaming card updates send full accumulated text instead of incremental diffs, fixing content truncation on Feishu
- Final close path uses `replaceCardContent` consistently instead of branching between append and replace

## Problem

Since 2026.5.20, `resolveStreamingCardAppendContent` was introduced to send only the delta between `sentText` and the current accumulated text. However, the Feishu CardKit API `PUT /cardkit/v1/cards/{id}/elements/{element_id}/content` **replaces** the entire element content rather than appending to it. This caused each streaming update to overwrite previous content, leaving only the last chunk visible in the card.

The bug is silent — Feishu returns HTTP 200 for each update, and the message preview sidebar shows the correct full text (from message metadata), but the card body only displays the final fragment.

## Root Cause

`resolveStreamingCardAppendContent` returns `nextText.slice(previousText.length)` when the new text starts with the previous text. This diff was passed to `updateCardContent`, which PUTs it as the full `content` value — so the card element is replaced with just the tail end.

## Fix

- **`update()` method**: Pass `mergedText` (full accumulated text) directly to `updateCardContent` instead of the incremental diff
- **`close()` method**: Always use `replaceCardContent` for the final update, removing the conditional append-vs-replace branch

This restores the behavior from 2026.5.12 and earlier, where each streaming update sent the complete text.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified locally on OpenClaw 2026.5.28 by patching the dist file
- [x] Sent multi-section streaming card messages via Feishu — all content sections display correctly
- [x] Confirmed short messages (<1KB) that were previously truncated now show in full

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: kylefu8
